palettes.name: Obsidian Brand
palettes.description: Official Obsidian Logic AI brand identity - Obsidian Black + Signal Yellow from design-tokens.json
palettes.color_theory: Obsidian Black (#0E0E0E) as the premium dark foundation paired with Signal Yellow (#F9C74F) for high-contrast authority. The warm gold accent creates premium positioning without corporate coldness. Light gold (#FCF0D5) softens secondary elements.
palettes.best_for: Brand-consistent posts, authority content, thought leadership, data-heavy infographics, company announcements

usage_guidelines.primary_use: Backgrounds, headline foundations, dark containers, primary text on light surfaces
usage_guidelines.secondary_use: Key data points, CTAs, accent highlights, important metrics, section headers
usage_guidelines.accent_use: Subtle warm highlights, secondary text on dark backgrounds, soft containers
usage_guidelines.neutral_use: Light backgrounds, card surfaces, breathing room, text containers

palettes.name: Executive Navy
palettes.description: Deep navy + crisp white + gold accent - the classic LinkedIn authority palette
palettes.color_theory: Navy conveys competence and trust on LinkedIn where blue is the native color. Crisp white text on navy creates maximum readability in the feed. Gold accent adds premium differentiation without competing with LinkedIn's own blue UI.
palettes.best_for: Thought leadership, C-suite content, professional frameworks, consulting insights, industry analysis

usage_guidelines.primary_use: Backgrounds, headline containers, primary text blocks, dark sections
usage_guidelines.secondary_use: Headlines on dark backgrounds, body text on light backgrounds, structural lines
usage_guidelines.accent_use: Key metrics, CTAs, data highlights, section dividers, important callouts
usage_guidelines.neutral_use: Light backgrounds, card surfaces, subtle containers, breathing room

palettes.name: Bold Coral
palettes.description: Warm coral + dark charcoal - scroll-stopping warmth that stands out in the LinkedIn feed
palettes.color_theory: Coral is one of the highest-engagement colors on LinkedIn because it contrasts sharply with the platform's blue/white/grey interface. The warm undertone creates approachability while the charcoal grounds it in professionalism. Amber accent bridges the warmth.
palettes.emotional_context: Engaging, warm, approachable, energetic, human, scroll-stopping
palettes.best_for: Storytelling posts, engagement hooks, personal narratives, hot takes, community content, promotional CTAs

usage_guidelines.primary_use: Headlines, key callouts, CTAs, visual anchors, attention-grabbing elements
usage_guidelines.secondary_use: Body text, structural elements, supporting containers, dark backgrounds
usage_guidelines.accent_use: Secondary highlights, warm icons, supporting data points, decorative elements
usage_guidelines.neutral_use: Light warm backgrounds, card surfaces, breathing room, text containers

palettes.name: Forest & Cream
palettes.description: Deep emerald + warm cream - premium, distinctive, rarely seen on LinkedIn
palettes.color_theory: Deep green is one of the most underused colors on LinkedIn, making it instantly distinctive in the feed. It signals growth, stability, and wealth. Paired with warm cream it feels premium and editorial. Gold accent adds a luxury touch.
palettes.emotional_context: Premium, distinctive, growth-oriented, stable, editorial, sophisticated
palettes.best_for: Educational frameworks, growth metrics, ROI content, case studies, premium positioning, evergreen advice

usage_guidelines.primary_use: Backgrounds, headline containers, primary structural elements, dark sections
usage_guidelines.secondary_use: Text on dark backgrounds, light containers, body text areas, cream surfaces
usage_guidelines.accent_use: Key metrics, CTAs, important highlights, premium data callouts, icons
usage_guidelines.neutral_use: Page backgrounds, subtle containers, text breathing room, card surfaces

palettes.name: Clean Slate
palettes.description: Cool grey-blue + white + electric blue accent - modern SaaS aesthetic for tech content
palettes.color_theory: Slate grey reads as modern and tech-forward without the overused navy. The cool blue accent aligns with LinkedIn's native color language for trust, while being distinct enough to stand out. Clean white creates maximum readability.
palettes.emotional_context: Modern, clean, tech-savvy, data-driven, focused, professional
palettes.best_for: Tech content, data visualizations, SaaS messaging, process breakdowns, developer-adjacent content, product announcements

usage_guidelines.primary_use: Backgrounds, text blocks, dark containers, headline foundations
usage_guidelines.secondary_use: Headlines on dark backgrounds, body text on light backgrounds, structural whites
usage_guidelines.accent_use: Interactive elements, CTAs, key data highlights, links, important metrics
usage_guidelines.neutral_use: Light backgrounds, card surfaces, subtle section dividers, breathing room

palettes.name: Midnight Pop
palettes.description: Near-black + vibrant accent colors - high-contrast dark mode that dominates the feed
palettes.color_theory: Dark backgrounds dominate LinkedIn feeds because most content is white. A near-black canvas makes any content immediately visible. Vibrant purple accent is rare on LinkedIn and creates instant visual distinction. Light grey text avoids harsh pure-white glare.
palettes.emotional_context: Bold, disruptive, modern, high-contrast, attention-commanding, innovative
palettes.best_for: Viral hooks, contrarian takes, bold statements, trend commentary, provocative content, data reveals

usage_guidelines.primary_use: Full backgrounds, dark containers, primary canvas, structural foundation
usage_guidelines.secondary_use: Body text, headlines on dark backgrounds, structural lines, readable text
usage_guidelines.accent_use: Key highlights, CTAs, data callouts, visual anchors, important metrics, icons
usage_guidelines.neutral_use: Subtle containers within dark backgrounds, card separation, secondary sections
